ddls:
CREATE TABLE "size_specifications" (
  "size_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each size category */,
  "size_name" TEXT /* Name of the size (e.g., S, M, L, XL, XXL) */,
  "size_description" TEXT /* Description of the size (e.g., Small, Medium) */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Target gender for the size (e.g., Male, Female) */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Target age group (e.g., Adult, Child) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("size_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement type */,
  "measurement_name" TEXT /* Name of the measurement (e.g., Shirt Length, Chest, Stomach) */,
  "unit_of_measure" TEXT /* Unit of measurement (e.g., cm, inches) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "size_measurements" (
  "size_measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each size measurement entry */,
  "size_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the size category (S, M, L, XL, XXL) */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the measurement type (Shirt Length, Chest, etc.) */,
  "measurement_value" REAL /* Value of the measurement for the specific size */,
  "tolerance" REAL /* Acceptable tolerance range for the measurement */,
  "customizable" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the measurement is customizable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("size_measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_size_measurements_size_id FOREIGN KEY ("size_id") REFERENCES size_specifications ("size_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_size_measurements_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES measurements ("measurement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each clothing product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the clothing product */,
  "category" TEXT /* Type/category of the clothing (e.g., shirt, pants) */,
  "brand" TEXT /* Brand of the product */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the product */,
  "target_market" TEXT /* Target market (e.g., Europe, Asia) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_sizes" (
  "product_size_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product size entry */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product */,
  "size_id" INTEGER /* ID of the available size for the product */,
  "inventory_count" INTEGER /* Number of items available in this size */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the inventory count */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_size_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_sizes_size_id FOREIGN KEY ("size_id") REFERENCES size_specifications ("size_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_sizes_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., designer, production manager, admin) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the sizing information */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access occurred */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Data/content of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., Sizing Trends, Fit Feedback) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., Draft, Finalized) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
